rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO PLACER COUNTY** IN THE MATTER OF THE JOHN JAMES TRACY TRUST DATED 10/7/1999 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Costs
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Before the court is Petitioner Rose Tracy Reinheimer's Motion to Strike All Costs, referring to a costs memorandum filed by Respondent Vittorio Tracy. Tracy's Memorandum of Costs was not filed timely under CRC 3.1700(a)(1). However, in its discretion, the court will extend the period for filing by five days and consider the filed memorandum. (See (CRC 3.1700(b)(3); Adam v. De Charon (1995) 31 Cal. App. 4th 708, 713.) Cardinal Health 301, Inc. v. Tyco Elecs. Corp. (2008) 169 Cal. App. 4th 116, 155.) The record before the court supports a finding that Reinheimer has standing to prosecute this motion. Reinheimer's Motion to Strike All Costs is DENIED. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 1032(b), a "prevailing party" who falls within at least one of the four statutory categories is automatically entitled to recover costs "as a matter of right," unless otherwise provided by statute. (CCP section 1032(a); Charton v. Harkey (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 730, 738-739.) The statutory categories include a defendant in whose favor a dismissal is entered, whether the dismissal is voluntary or involuntary, or with or without prejudice. (CCP section 1032(a)(4); Mon Chong Loong Trading Corp. v Sup. Ct. (2013) 218 CA4th 87, 93-94; Santisas v. Goodin (1998) 17 Cal.4th 599, 606; Cano v. Glover (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 326, 328, 331.) Here, the court entered a dismissal on each of the three accounting petitions that were the subject of Tracy's August 24, 2020 Section 998 Offer. This is a dismissal in Tracy's favor. He is entitled to recovery of costs incurred in connection with these petitions "as a matter of right." Reinheimer has not established that Tracy's memorandum of costs was premature. Dismissals were entered on the three accounting petitions, starting the clock for costs memorandum and related filings. Reinheimer has not shown that her acceptance of the May 12, 2021 Section 998 Offer (which "renewed" a separate and independent 998 offer made on August 24, 2020) precludes Tracy's recovery of costs on the three accounting petitions. The May 12, 2021 Offer related to Tracy's Petition for Removal, a separate petition. That Offer made no reference to the accounting petitions and the August 24, 2020 Offer made no reference to the Petition for Removal. The August 24, 2020 Offer provided for the parties to bear their own fees and costs as to the Petition for Removal only, and, again, made no reference to the accounting petitions. Reinheimer has not demonstrated that Tracy's memorandum of costs is precluded under the collateral estoppel doctrine. Prior to the hearing, Tracy shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on September 8, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
